Josue Duverger.bio: Haitian goalkeeper who has built a solid career in American lower-division football with Louisville City in the USL Championship. A dependable presence between the posts for Les Grenadiers.
Josue Duverger.playingStyle: Reliable shot-stopper with good reflexes and vocal leadership from the back, comfortable organizing his defense
Josue Duverger.worldCup2026Role: First-choice goalkeeper for Haiti's historic World Cup campaign
Josue Duverger.notableFact: Has been a key player for Louisville City in the USL Championship, one of the top clubs in American second-tier football

Carlens Arcus.bio: Right-back who has established himself in French football with Rodez in Ligue 2. One of the more experienced defenders in the Haitian squad with 35 caps, providing stability on the right flank.
Carlens Arcus.playingStyle: Disciplined full-back who balances defensive duties with the ability to support attacks down the right side
Carlens Arcus.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back providing experience and defensive solidity
Carlens Arcus.notableFact: One of the few Haitian internationals playing in French professional football

Alex Christian.bio: Center-back who has gained experience in Central American football with Real Esteli in Nicaragua. A physical defender who adds depth to Haiti's backline options.
Alex Christian.playingStyle: Strong, physical center-back who relies on his aerial presence and commitment in defensive duels
Alex Christian.worldCup2026Role: Defensive depth option providing cover at center-back
Alex Christian.notableFact: Has gained valuable international club experience playing in Central American football competitions

Mechack Jerome.bio: Veteran defender with extensive MLS experience at CF Montreal, one of the most capped players in the current Haitian squad with 45 caps. A seasoned leader who brings calm and organization to the defense.
Mechack Jerome.playingStyle: Experienced, commanding center-back who reads the game well and marshals the defense with his leadership
Mechack Jerome.worldCup2026Role: Senior defensive leader and likely starting center-back
Mechack Jerome.notableFact: Has spent many years in MLS with Montreal, becoming one of the most experienced defenders in Haitian football history

Ricardo Ade.bio: Young center-back playing in MLS for Houston Dynamo, representing the newer generation of Haitian defenders. A physically imposing presence who has been developing rapidly at club level.
Ricardo Ade.playingStyle: Athletic, modern center-back with good recovery speed and strength in one-on-one defensive situations
Ricardo Ade.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back option bringing youth and athleticism to the defense
Ricardo Ade.notableFact: Playing in MLS with Houston Dynamo has given him experience against high-quality opposition on a weekly basis

Danley Jean Jacques.bio: Central midfielder playing domestically for Violette AC in the Ligue Haitienne. One of the homegrown talents in the squad representing the strength of Haiti's domestic league.
Danley Jean Jacques.playingStyle: Energetic midfielder who covers ground well and contributes both defensively and in build-up play
Danley Jean Jacques.worldCup2026Role: Midfield option providing energy and depth from domestic football
Danley Jean Jacques.notableFact: Represents the domestic Haitian football pipeline as a key player for Violette AC, one of Haiti's most successful clubs

Derrick Etienne Jr.bio: One of Haiti's most important attackers, born in the United States to Haitian parents. Has built a strong career in MLS with Columbus Crew and is Haiti's go-to creative threat on the left wing with 40 caps and 7 goals.
Derrick Etienne Jr.playingStyle: Tricky, creative winger with good dribbling skills and an eye for goal, effective cutting inside from the left
Derrick Etienne Jr.worldCup2026Role: Key attacking player on the left wing, one of Haiti's primary creative outlets
Derrick Etienne Jr.notableFact: Born in New York to Haitian immigrant parents, he chose to represent Haiti over the United States

Zachary Herivaux.bio: Central midfielder who has been part of the American lower-division football scene with Rio Grande Valley FC. A steady presence in Haiti's midfield with 25 caps.
Zachary Herivaux.playingStyle: Workmanlike central midfielder who keeps things ticking with simple, effective passing and good positional sense
Zachary Herivaux.worldCup2026Role: Midfield rotation player providing balance and stability in the center
Zachary Herivaux.notableFact: Has been a consistent performer in the USL Championship, gaining experience in competitive American football

Duckens Nazon.bio: Haiti's all-time leading active goalscorer with 15 goals in 45 caps, Nazon has been the focal point of the national team's attack for years. Has played across Europe including stints in Belgium and England.
Duckens Nazon.playingStyle: Clinical finisher with good movement in the box and the ability to score with both feet and his head
Duckens Nazon.worldCup2026Role: Experienced striker option providing a proven goal threat at international level
Duckens Nazon.notableFact: Haiti's most prolific active international goalscorer, having scored crucial goals in World Cup qualifying and CONCACAF competitions

Frantzdy Pierrot.bio: Powerful striker who stands at 193cm, providing a physical aerial presence that few defenders in CONCACAF can match. Has played in Turkey's Super Lig with Goztepe after experience in French football.
Frantzdy Pierrot.playingStyle: Target man with exceptional aerial ability who holds the ball up well and brings teammates into play
Frantzdy Pierrot.worldCup2026Role: Starting striker and primary target man, the focal point of Haiti's attack
Frantzdy Pierrot.notableFact: His imposing 193cm frame makes him one of the tallest strikers at the 2026 World Cup

Stephane Lambese.bio: Left winger who has been a part of Haiti's national team setup for several years. Currently a free agent but brings experience and familiarity with the international setup.
Stephane Lambese.playingStyle: Pacey winger who uses his speed to stretch defenses and deliver crosses from the left flank
Stephane Lambese.worldCup2026Role: Squad depth on the wing providing cover and a different option in attack
Stephane Lambese.notableFact: A long-serving member of the Haitian national team who has contributed across multiple qualification campaigns

Johnny Placide.bio: The most experienced goalkeeper in the Haitian squad with 40 caps and a career spent mostly in French football with Guingamp. At 38, he is the elder statesman of the team.
Johnny Placide.playingStyle: Experienced, calm goalkeeper who brings composure and organization to the defense with his commanding presence
Johnny Placide.worldCup2026Role: Veteran backup goalkeeper and mentor to the younger keepers in the squad
Johnny Placide.notableFact: Has spent the bulk of his career in French football, one of the most experienced Haitian players ever at this level

Ronaldo Damus.bio: Young striker who has been prolific in youth and college football in the United States before moving to FC Zurich in the Swiss Super League. A natural goalscorer with a bright future.
Ronaldo Damus.playingStyle: Mobile, sharp striker who makes intelligent runs and finishes well in and around the six-yard box
Ronaldo Damus.worldCup2026Role: Attacking rotation option bringing youth, energy, and a keen goal instinct
Ronaldo Damus.notableFact: Won numerous college football awards in the United States before making the jump to European professional football

Steven Seance.bio: Midfielder who has spent his career in French lower-league football with AS Beauvais. A consistent contributor to the Haitian national team over several years.
Steven Seance.playingStyle: Steady central midfielder with good technical ability and experience in French football's competitive lower divisions
Steven Seance.worldCup2026Role: Experienced midfield depth option for key squad rotation
Steven Seance.notableFact: Has been a steady presence in French amateur and semi-professional football for over a decade

Bryan Alceus.bio: Young center-back developing in the MLS system with Philadelphia Union II in MLS Next Pro. Represents the emerging generation of Haitian-American defenders.
Bryan Alceus.playingStyle: Athletic young defender who uses his pace and physicality to compete in aerial and ground duels
Bryan Alceus.worldCup2026Role: Young defensive prospect providing cover and future potential at center-back
Bryan Alceus.notableFact: Part of the Philadelphia Union development pathway, training alongside MLS first-team players

Nathaniel Saint-Felix.bio: Central midfielder based in the USL Championship who is building his international career with Haiti. A technically capable player in the engine room.
Nathaniel Saint-Felix.playingStyle: Neat, tidy midfielder who keeps possession well and looks to distribute the ball quickly and efficiently
Nathaniel Saint-Felix.worldCup2026Role: Midfield depth option capable of contributing in a holding or box-to-box role
Nathaniel Saint-Felix.notableFact: One of several Haitian players gaining professional experience in the growing American soccer pyramid

Soni Mustivar.bio: Veteran defensive midfielder who has spent many years in MLS with Sporting Kansas City. Born in France to Haitian parents, he is one of the most experienced players in the squad with 30 caps.
Soni Mustivar.playingStyle: Tough, combative defensive midfielder who shields the backline with aggressive ball-winning and physical presence
Soni Mustivar.worldCup2026Role: Key defensive midfielder tasked with protecting the back four and breaking up opposition attacks
Soni Mustivar.notableFact: Born in France and built a long MLS career with Sporting Kansas City before committing to Haiti at international level

Emmanuel Riviere.bio: Former Newcastle United striker who represented France at youth level before switching to Haiti. Has played in England, France, Italy, and Turkey during a well-traveled career.
Emmanuel Riviere.playingStyle: Experienced target forward who uses his movement and experience to create space and score goals
Emmanuel Riviere.worldCup2026Role: Veteran attacking option bringing top-level European experience to the squad
Emmanuel Riviere.notableFact: Played in the Premier League for Newcastle United and has represented both France youth teams and Haiti at senior level

James Marcelin.bio: One of the oldest players in the squad at 37, Marcelin is a veteran midfielder who has spent years in the USL Championship. A long-serving member of the national team with 20 caps.
James Marcelin.playingStyle: Experienced midfielder who uses game intelligence and positioning to compensate for declining pace
James Marcelin.worldCup2026Role: Senior squad member providing leadership, experience, and midfield depth
James Marcelin.notableFact: One of Haiti's most loyal and long-serving players, having represented the country for well over a decade

Andrew Jean-Baptiste.bio: Tall, imposing center-back who has spent most of his career in MLS with teams including Portland Timbers and Colorado Rapids. Born in the United States to Haitian parents.
Andrew Jean-Baptiste.playingStyle: Towering center-back who dominates in the air and uses his physical attributes to win defensive battles
Andrew Jean-Baptiste.worldCup2026Role: Defensive option for set-piece situations and physical matches requiring aerial superiority
Andrew Jean-Baptiste.notableFact: Standing at 193cm, he is one of the tallest outfield players in the Haitian squad

Alex Junior.bio: Left-back playing domestically for Arcahaie FC in the Ligue Haitienne. One of the players representing Haiti's domestic football in the World Cup squad.
Alex Junior.playingStyle: Attack-minded left-back who contributes to build-up play and provides width on the left flank
Alex Junior.worldCup2026Role: Backup left-back providing a homegrown option on the left side of defense
Alex Junior.notableFact: One of several domestically-based players representing the Ligue Haitienne at the World Cup

Louis Labaze.bio: Third-choice goalkeeper playing for Violette AC in the Ligue Haitienne. Part of Haiti's goalkeeping depth as they embark on their World Cup journey.
Louis Labaze.playingStyle: Solid domestic goalkeeper with good handling and communication skills
Louis Labaze.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper providing squad depth and emergency cover
Louis Labaze.notableFact: Plays for Violette AC, one of the most decorated and popular clubs in Haitian football

Carlin Isidor.bio: The most valuable player in Haiti's squad, Isidor plays for Zenit St Petersburg in the Russian Premier League. Born in France to Haitian parents, he brings European top-flight experience.
Carlin Isidor.playingStyle: Quick, dynamic striker with sharp movement and clinical finishing ability in and around the penalty area
Carlin Isidor.worldCup2026Role: Key attacking threat and potential game-changer off the bench or in the starting lineup
Carlin Isidor.notableFact: Haiti's highest-valued player, having earned a move to Russian giants Zenit St Petersburg

Steeven Saba.bio: Homegrown midfielder who plays for Violette AC in the Ligue Haitienne. One of the younger domestic talents selected for the World Cup squad.
Steeven Saba.playingStyle: Energetic central midfielder who contributes with his running and ability to link play in the middle of the park
Steeven Saba.worldCup2026Role: Young domestic midfielder providing squad depth and future potential
Steeven Saba.notableFact: A product of Haitian domestic football making his mark on the international stage

Leverton Pierre.bio: Young right-back from AS Capoise in the Ligue Haitienne, one of the youngest and least experienced players in the squad. Represents Haiti's investment in its footballing future.
Leverton Pierre.playingStyle: Young, energetic full-back who uses his pace and enthusiasm to contribute both defensively and in attack
Leverton Pierre.worldCup2026Role: Young squad player gaining experience at the highest level for future campaigns
Leverton Pierre.notableFact: At just 25, he is one of the youngest players selected from Haiti's domestic league for the World Cup

Wilde-Donald Guerrier.bio: The most capped player in the current squad with 55 appearances and 10 goals, Guerrier is a Haitian football legend. A veteran winger who has served his country faithfully for well over a decade.
Wilde-Donald Guerrier.playingStyle: Experienced, clever winger who uses his knowledge and positioning to find space and create chances
Wilde-Donald Guerrier.worldCup2026Role: Senior squad leader and talisman whose experience is invaluable in a World Cup setting
Wilde-Donald Guerrier.notableFact: Haiti's most capped player in the current squad and one of the nation's all-time most decorated footballers
